creator: mdh@solitox.net
text:
When making targets 'install' and 'reinstall' in the graphics/gimp port, the output contains an error from /bin/sh.  

(root@vesper) [/usr/ports/graphics/gimp]: (make deinstall && make reinstall)
===>  Deinstalling for graphics/gimp
===>   Deinstalling gimp-2.2,2
===>  Installing for gimp-2.2,2
===>   gimp-2.2,2 depends on executable in : gimp-2.2 - found
===>   gimp-2.2,2 depends on package: gimp-help>0 - found
===>   gimp-2.2,2 depends on file: /usr/local/libexec/gimp/2.2/plug-ins/print - found
===>   Generating temporary packing list
===>  Checking if graphics/gimp already installed
===>   Registering installation for gimp-2.2,2
/bin/sh:Argument list too long
(root@vesper) [/usr/ports/graphics/gimp]: 

cvsup'd ports 20070427, error still occurs.  I'm not sure if this effects anything negatively; I haven't noticed any problems, but I'm far from a hard-core user of The GIMP.
